assessor_raw:
## Baseline Assessment: Sequence Model vs. Gradient Boosting for Card Fraud Detection

This design compares a sequence LSTM/transformer against gradient-boosted features for real-time card fraud detection.

**Issue 1 — Temporal leakage from random split:** The design uses a random 70/15/15 stratified split across 24 months of transaction data. Card fraud patterns evolve chronologically — new fraud tactics emerge over time, and fraud rings rotate through merchant categories. A random split places transactions from month 22 into training alongside transactions from month 3 in test. The model learns from future fraud patterns (which may already be in its training set) and then is evaluated on past transactions. This is temporal leakage that inflates apparent performance. A chronological split is required.

**Issue 2 — PR-AUC vs. recall at 5% FPR:** The design explicitly records the 5% FPR threshold and carries it forward to test evaluation. The operational question is: at that 5% FPR operating point, what fraction of fraud is detected? That is recall at 5% FPR. PR-AUC aggregates over all thresholds, including those never used operationally. A model with better PR-AUC at thresholds corresponding to 50% FPR is not operationally better at the deployed 5% FPR threshold. Recall at 5% FPR should be primary or co-primary.
